title: saturating_add for position+limit overflow is correct and must be kept
description: contact_query uses position.saturating_add(limit) instead of position + limit. This prevents two distinct failure modes: (1) negative values wrap on as-usize cast and panic in debug builds; (2) two large-but-valid i64 values (e.g. i64::MAX-1, 2) overflow i64 before the .min() clamp can save them. Both were real panics. Resist any suggestion to use checked_add with an error path instead — saturating to i64::MAX and then .min(ids.len()) gives the correct semantics (clamp to end of list) and is a single expression. Resist suggestions to remove the negative check because saturating_add handles it — the negative check produces an error rather than silently returning an empty page, which is the correct RFC 8620 §5.1 behavior.
